rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intermolecular [2+1+n] annulation of alkenes with diazo compounds and tert-butyl nitrite to give isoxazolines (with styrenes) or 5,6-dihydro-4H-1,2-oxazines (with allylbenzenes), where diazo compounds serve as radical acceptors
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of tBuONO generates an electron-rich nitroso radical (·NO) and tert-butoxide; the resulting C-centered benzylic radical is oxidized at the anode to a benzylic cation (radical-polar crossover); paired electrolysis with H2 evolution avoided by using tBuONO as sacrificial species

key_intermediates.name: nitroso radical (·NO) from homolysis/heterolysis of tBuONO
α-oxime-ester C-radical I (·NO addition to diazo with N2 loss)
benzylic radical II from addition of I to styrene
benzylic cation III after anodic oxidation of II
alkyl radical V (from addition of I to allylbenzene)
more-stable benzyl radical VI after 1,2-aryl migration
nitrile oxide 6 (alternative anodic oxidation product of I, not observed)

electron_flow: Cathode reduces tBuONO to give ·NO and tBuO-/tBuO·; ·NO adds to the diazo compound with loss of N2 to give an α-acyl C-radical (I), which adds to the alkene to give a benzylic/alkyl radical (II or V); for allylbenzenes, 1,2-aryl migration gives the more stable benzyl radical VI; anodic oxidation of the C-radical to a cation, tautomerization to the oxime, and intramolecular O-attack/dehydration closes the isoxazoline or 1,2-oxazine ring.

probe_reagents.name: H-type divided cell experiment
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a still formed in 45% GC yield in divided cell (vs 75% undivided), indicating tBuONO undergoes homolytic cleavage independently and confirming both anodic and cathodic events contribute

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of tBuONO with/without HFIP
probe_reagents.observation: HFIP lowers reduction potential of tBuONO to -0.78 V vs Ag/AgCl, rationalizing why HFIP facilitates nitroso radical generation

probe_reagents.name: allylbenzene substrates (radical clock / migration probe)
probe_reagents.observation: 1,2-aryl migration observed to give 5,6-dihydro-4H-1,2-oxazines (R2=H, alkyl), proving radical pathway rather than 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition (which would give isoxazoline 7, not observed)

probe_reagents.name: absence of nitrile oxide [3+2] product 7
probe_reagents.observation: Only [2+1+3] product 5 formed, not the cycloaddition product 7 expected from nitrile oxide 6, ruling out anodic oxidation of I to nitrile oxide / 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition pathway

notes: Authors explicitly rule out the alternative 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition pathway (anodic oxidation of radical I to nitrile oxide 6 followed by [3+2] with alkene) on the basis that allylbenzenes give 1,2-oxazines via aryl migration rather than the cycloaddition product 7. HFIP additive critical for lowering tBuONO reduction potential. Paired electrolysis: anode oxidizes benzylic radical, cathode reduces tBuONO — no sacrificial H2 evolution needed.
